text.title: Prompt Optimization for Token Reduction
text.summary: PromptOptimizer: token counting with tiktoken, whitespace compression, redundant phrase removal, and system prompt tightening — with explicit limits on what must not be compressed.

rule.statement: Compress only user-facing inputs and system prompts; never compress few-shot examples, agent scratchpad, or chain-of-thought steps.
rule.rationale: Few-shot examples depend on precise phrasing and spacing to demonstrate the target format. Compressing them produces a model that learns from corrupted demonstrations. CoT compression breaks reasoning chains.

rule.statement: Run compression on the system prompt first, where savings are largest — a 500-token system prompt that compresses to 350 saves 150 tokens on every request.
rule.rationale: System prompt tokens are paid on every call; user message tokens are paid once per turn. System prompt optimization has higher ROI.

antipattern.description: Aggressive compression that strips semantic information from instructions (e.g., removing examples or detail from JSON schemas).
antipattern.reason: Losing constraint details produces outputs that do not match the required format; quality loss outweighs token savings.

rule.statement: Count tokens with tiktoken before sending any expensive call; fall back to a cheaper model if the prompt exceeds a budget threshold.
rule.rationale: Token count determines cost and latency; routing before the call is cheaper than paying for an oversized request and then retrying with a smaller model.

example.code:
import tiktoken
def is_within_budget(text: str, model: str = "gpt-4o", max_tokens: int = 4000) -> bool: enc = tiktoken.encoding_for_model(model) return len(enc.encode(text)) <= max_tokens
# Route by token budget before calling model = "gpt-4o" if is_within_budget(prompt, max_tokens=2000) else "gpt-4o-mini"
example.note: Rule-based routing — no LLM call needed for the routing decision itself.

rule.statement: Use Anthropic prompt caching (cache_control) for system prompts exceeding 1024 tokens — the 90% read discount is immediate with no code changes beyond adding the header.
rule.rationale: Anthropic caches up to 4 system prompt breakpoints; repeated calls reuse the cached prefix at 90% cost reduction, making this the highest-ROI optimization for Claude-based apps.
